title: Erdős #1052 companion infrastructure: p-free unitary divisors
informal_statement: Erdos #1052 (open): are there finitely many unitary perfect numbers? This packet proves only bounded companion infrastructure toward the known (Subbarao-Warren 1966) result that unitary perfect numbers are even -- NOT the open problem itself and NOT yet the full even_of_isUnitaryPerfect headline theorem. Continues frontier.erdos.erdos_1052_proper_eq_erase.v1 and erdos_1052_not_dvd_div_ordproj.v1: proves the p-free unitary divisors of n are exactly the unitary divisors of the p-free part ordCompl[p] n.
informal_proof_idea: Forward direction: a p-free unitary divisor e of n is coprime to n's full p-part (since e is coprime to n/e and p-free), hence divides the p-free part ordCompl[p] n directly; coprimality of e with (ordCompl[p] n)/e follows since that quotient divides n/e. Backward direction: a unitary divisor e of the p-free part is itself p-free (else it would divide n's full p-part, contradicting Nat.not_dvd_ordCompl) and divides n; coprimality with n/e follows by combining coprimality with the p-part (automatic, since e is p-free) and with (ordCompl[p] n)/e (given).
formal_statement_pp: theorem filter_not_dvd_eq_uDiv_ordCompl {n p : ℕ} (hn : n ≠ 0) (hp : p.Prime) : (uDiv n).filter (fun d => ¬ p ∣ d) = uDiv (ordCompl[p] n)

review.review_basis: repo self-review; kernel-verified through the tracked proof-search loop; statement cross-checked against the sibling repo's already-kernel_verified proof of the same result

notes: ANTI-OVERCLAIM: this packet proves only bounded companion infrastructure, not Erdos #1052 itself (genuinely OPEN) and not the full known Subbarao-Warren 1966 companion result (unitary perfect numbers are even) -- that headline theorem still additionally needs sum_uDiv_factor (a Finset.sum_bij' 5-argument bijection with deeply nested bullets) and sum_uDiv_even (a Finset.sum_involution 4-argument involution), both deferred to a future cycle as the genuinely hard remaining pieces. Proof authority: Lean kernel via the tracked proof-search loop (episode 32ac25bb, problem_version 93ac7bac-dcbc-46d1-90c2-39bfbe11c157), submitted as a SubmitModule (1 def uDiv + 1 helper theorem mem_uDiv + root theorem filter_not_dvd_eq_uDiv_ordCompl), kernel_verified on the FIRST attempt. The original's `constructor` + two `.`-bullet Iff case splits were rewritten as a single Iff.intro term with each direction's multi-step body wrapped in its own parenthesized by-block, reusing the exact template that worked for erdos_1052_proper_eq_erase.v1's Iff split. Independently re-verified in THIS environment's own pinned toolchain rather than treated as evidence merely because the sibling repo already reports it kernel_verified there -- per this repo's evidence policy. training.eligibility: quarantined per the default-quarantine policy for open_problem_related packets pending real external review.
